rejected_readings.label: contrast_after_decay
rejected_readings.rendering: The sounds that remain after vibration ceases carry the long and prolated vowel distinctions that identify words.
rejected_readings.why: Reverses the acoustic division the commentary insists on: contrastive properties (vowel length) belong to the driven phase, not the free decay. The decay phase marks only stylistic delivery, never contrastive identity. This inverts the relation's direction.

rejected_readings.label: decay_phase_contrast
rejected_readings.rendering: The long and prolated vowel distinctions belong to the post-vibration resonances that differentiate utterance styles.
rejected_readings.why: Assigns contrastive vowel properties to the decay phase, when the commentary explicitly locates them in the amplified driven phase. This demotes a contrastive property to a stylistic one (sort error), making the axiom unlicensed.

notes: The verse formalizes the acoustic foundation of the contrast/style distinction (1.76-77): driven vs. decaying oscillation. The practical Dravya (substance) is what is struck, but treated here as absolute ground. 'Contrast' as a property name abstracts the semantic function carried by vowel length distinctions; it is licensed by the commentary's language 'never which word it was'.
